docs.title: Zubbl SDK
docs.text:
Make any AI agent 20-50% smarter automatically.

Drop-in SDK that adds trajectory learning, failure recovery, and continuous improvement to any agent framework. No infrastructure to manage.

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text:
pip install zubbl-sdk

from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx")
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(your_agent)
result = smart_agent.run("Complete this task")

That's it. Your agent now learns from every execution, recovers from failures, and improves over time.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
graph LR
    A[Your Agent] --> B[Zubbl SDK]
    B --> C[Execute Task]
    C --> D{Success?}
    D -->|Yes| E[Learn Pattern]
    D -->|No| F[Reflect + Retry]
    F --> C
    E --> G[Improve Policies]
    G --> A
  1. Record — SDK captures every step your agent takes
  2. Learn — Patterns are extracted and scored automatically
  3. Improve — Next run gets better recommendations
  4. Recover — Failed tasks are retried with learned insights

docs.title: Core Features
docs.text: Feature What It Does Trajectory Recording Auto-captures steps, tools, tokens, latency Policy Recommendations Get optimized strategies before each task Failure Recovery ~33% of failures auto-recovered on first retry Improvement Tracking Measure exactly how much your agents improved

docs.title: Cloud Features (Pro plans)
docs.text: Feature What It Does Skill Graph Map skill dependencies and find gaps Vector Search Find similar past trajectories semantically Training Continuous policy optimization Real-time Streams Live monitoring of agent activity

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text:
REST API documentation for the Zubbl backend.

Base URL: https://api.zubbl.ai

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text:
All requests require a Bearer token in the Authorization header:

Authorization: Bearer zubbl_<tenant_id>_<secret>

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text:
Check API status.

GET /health

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
{
  "status": "healthy",
  "version": "0.1.0",
  "timestamp": "2026-02-18T10:30:00Z"
}

docs.title: Ingest Trajectory
docs.text:
Ingest a trajectory for learning.

POST /ingest

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "trajectory_id": "traj_abc123def456",
  "tenant_id": "tenant_001",
  "task_description": "Write a Python function to sort a list",
  "steps": [
    {
      "step_id": "step_1",
      "action": "analyze_requirements",
      "tool_name": null,
      "tool_input": null,
      "tool_output": null,
      "reasoning": "Need to understand sorting requirements",
      "tokens_used": 150,
      "latency_ms": 200
    },
    {
      "step_id": "step_2",
      "action": "write_code",
      "tool_name": "code_generator",
      "tool_input": {"language": "python"},
      "tool_output": "def sort_list(items): return sorted(items)",
      "tokens_used": 300,
      "latency_ms": 500
    }
  ],
  "status": "success",
  "total_tokens": 450,
  "total_latency_ms": 700,
  "metadata": {
    "agent": "my_agent",
    "version": "1.0"
  }
}

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
{
  "trajectory_id": "traj_abc123def456",
  "status": "accepted",
  "message": "Trajectory ingested successfully and queued for learning"
}

docs.title: Status Codes
docs.text: Code Description 200 Success 401 Unauthorized 422 Validation error 500 Server error

docs.title: Query Policy
docs.text:
Get policy recommendations for a task.

GET /query

docs.title: Query Parameters
docs.text: Parameter Type Required Description task string Yes Task description context string No Additional context (JSON string) rl_mode string No RL mode: local, cloud

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text: GET /query?task=Write%20a%20Python%20sorting%20function&rl_mode=local

docs.title: Response (Policy Found)
docs.text:
{
  "policy_id": "pol_coding_a1b2c3d4",
  "task_type": "coding",
  "recommended_actions": [
    "analyze_requirements",
    "write_code",
    "test_code",
    "refine_if_needed"
  ],
  "confidence_score": 0.85,
  "rl_mode": "local",
  "message": "Policy found"
}

docs.title: Response (No Policy)
docs.text:
{
  "policy_id": null,
  "task_type": "coding",
  "recommended_actions": [],
  "confidence_score": 0.0,
  "rl_mode": "local",
  "message": "No policy found for this task type yet. Keep using Zubbl and it will learn!"
}

docs.title: Submit Feedback
docs.text:
Submit feedback on a trajectory.

POST /feedback

docs.title: Request Body
docs.text:
{
  "feedback_id": "fb_xyz789",
  "trajectory_id": "traj_abc123def456",
  "rating": 0.8,
  "comment": "Good solution but could be more efficient",
  "source": "human"
}

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
{
  "feedback_id": "fb_xyz789",
  "status": "accepted",
  "message": "Feedback recorded and queued for processing"
}

docs.title: Get Usage
docs.text:
Get usage statistics for the current tenant.

GET /usage

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
{
  "tenant_id": "tenant_001",
  "period": "month",
  "trajectories_ingested": 1250,
  "queries_made": 3400,
  "rl_cycles_run": 890,
  "credits_used": 5000,
  "credits_remaining": 95000,
  "task_success_rate": 0.72,
  "avg_improvement": 0.23
}

docs.title: Get Job Status
docs.text:
Check the status of a processing job.

GET /job/{job_id}

docs.title: Response
docs.text:
{
  "job_id": "traj_abc123def456",
  "status": "completed"
}

docs.title: Possible Statuses
docs.text: Status Description pending Job is queued processing Job is being processed completed Job finished successfully failed Job failed

docs.title: Error Responses
docs.text:
All errors follow this format:

{
  "error": "Error message here"
}

docs.title: Common Error Codes
docs.text: Code Description 400 Bad Request — Invalid parameters 401 Unauthorized — Missing or invalid token 402 Payment Required — Insufficient credits 404 Not Found — Resource doesn't exist 429 Too Many Requests — Rate limit exceeded 500 Internal Server Error

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text:
Rate limits are enforced per tenant per minute:

Plan Limit Free 100/min Local PRO 1,000/min Cloud PRO 5,000/min Team 10,000/min

Rate limit headers:

X-RateLimit-Limit: 1000
X-RateLimit-Remaining: 950
X-RateLimit-Reset: 1708254000

docs.title: Webhooks (Coming Soon)
docs.text: Future support for webhooks on events: - trajectory.ingested - policy.updated - rl_cycle.completed

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text: Zubbl uses a dual learning loop to continuously improve your AI agents — without any code changes.

docs.title: The Learning Loop
docs.text:
graph TB
    A[Your Agent] -->|wrap once| B[Zubbl SDK]
    B -->|1. Record| C[Extract 3 Steps Automatically]
    C -->|2. Learn| D[Build Patterns by Category]
    D -->|3. Score| E[Rank by Success Rate]
    E -->|4. Policy| F[Build Recommendations]
    F -->|5. Inject| G[~30 Token Strategy Prefix]
    G -->|Next Run| A

    C -->|On Failure| H[Reflexion: Analyze Why]
    H -->|Partial Credit| D

docs.title: Step 1: Record (Zero Code Changes)
docs.text:
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(my_agent)  # One line. Never change again.
result = smart_agent("Write a sorting function")

Zubbl automatically extracts 3 steps from every call: - Understand task — detects task type (generate, debug, optimize, explain) - Execute — detects tools used from output (code_generator, debugger, etc.) - Validate — checks output quality

docs.title: Step 2: Extract Patterns
docs.text: Similar tasks accumulate patterns under the same category. "Write a function to sort" and "Write a function to reverse" both go under writing.

docs.title: Step 3: Score (Contrastive)
docs.text: Patterns are ranked by success rate. Successful approaches score higher than failed ones. Failed trajectories still contribute — steps that worked before the failure get partial credit.

docs.title: Step 4: Build Policy
docs.text:
After 2+ patterns per category, a policy is built with actionable recommendations: 

writing → planner, code_generator, quality_checker
debugging → planner, debugger, quality_checker

docs.title: Step 5: Inject (~30 Tokens)
docs.text:
Next time the agent runs a similar task, the learned strategy is prepended: 

[Strategy: planner→understand, code_generator→generate, checker→validate]
Write a function to sort a list

~30 tokens of overhead. The agent gets better without anyone changing any code.

docs.title: On Failure: Reflexion
docs.text: When an agent fails: 1. An LLM analyzes why it failed 2. The reflection is stored as a negative pattern 3. Steps that worked before the failure get partial credit 4. Next run avoids the same mistake

docs.title: Benchmark Results
docs.text: Metric Result Tasks to reach confidence 1.0 8 per category Actions learned per category 3 Token overhead ~30 tokens Code changes required Zero Success rate 100%

docs.title: Dashboard Feedback (No Code Changes)
docs.text:
1. Agent runs → trajectory recorded automatically
  2. Go to dashboard → click trajectory → rate it → add comment
  3. Feedback feeds into the learning loop
  4. Next run is better — no code changes

docs.title: Configuration
docs.text: Configuration options for the Zubbl SDK.

docs.title: SDK Configuration
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient, RLMode

client = ZubblClient(
    # Required
    api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx",

    # API endpoint
    base_url="https://api.zubbl.ai",  # Production (default)

    # RL Mode
    rl_mode=RLMode.LOCAL,      # Free tier
    # rl_mode=RLMode.COREWEAVE,  # Cloud PRO
    # rl_mode=RLMode.B200,       # Enterprise

    # Behavior
    auto_ingest=True,   # Auto-send successful trajectories
    auto_query=True,    # Auto-query before tasks
    local_cache=True,   # Cache policies locally

    # Performance
    timeout=30.0,       # Request timeout (seconds)
    retry_attempts=3,   # Retry count on failure
)

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Set these in your .env file or environment:

# Required
ZUBBL_API_KEY=zubbl_yourtenantid_yoursecret

# Optional — for LLM adapter
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

The SDK auto-loads .env via python-dotenv.

docs.title: RL Modes
docs.text: Mode Description Best For LOCAL Pattern matching + reflexion on your machine Development, testing COREWEAVE Cloud-powered learning with GPU acceleration Production B200 Enterprise-grade training High-volume production

docs.title: Credit Allocation
docs.text: Tier Price Cloud Credits Best For Free $0/mo 10K/mo Testing Local PRO $19/mo 25K/mo Indie hackers Cloud PRO $49/mo 100K/mo Startups Team $199/mo Unlimited Teams

docs.title: Credit Costs
docs.text: Operation Credits Trajectory ingest 1 Policy query (local) 0 Policy query (cloud) 10 Learning cycle (local) 0 Learning cycle (cloud) 100

docs.title: Getting Started
docs.text: Get Zubbl running in your project in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: From PyPI (Recommended)
docs.text: pip install zubbl-sdk

docs.title: From Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/zubbl/zubbl.git
cd zubbl
pip install -e .

docs.title: 1. Get Your API Key
docs.text:
Sign up at zubbl.ai to get your API key.

Format: zubbl_<tenant_id>_<secret>

docs.title: 2. Basic Integration (3 Lines!)
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

# Initialize client
zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_yourtenantid_yoursecret")

# Wrap your agent
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(your_agent)

# Use it exactly like before — now it learns!
result = smart_agent.run("Complete this task")

That's it! Your agent now uses Zubbl's dual learning loop: - Trajectory learning: Records executions, extracts patterns, serves cross-task policies - Reflexion: On failure, generates self-reflection and retries immediately - Gets measurably smarter over time (82% -> 88% on eval benchmarks)

docs.title: LangGraph
docs.text:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent
from zubbl import ZubblClient

# Your existing LangGraph agent
model = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o")
tools = [your_tools]
agent = create_react_agent(model, tools)

# Add Zubbl — one line
zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(agent)

# Use as normal
result = smart_agent.invoke({"messages": [("user", "Hello!")]})

docs.title: CrewAI
docs.text:
from crewai import Crew, Agent, Task
from zubbl import ZubblClient

# Your existing CrewAI setup
crew = Crew(
    agents=[researcher, writer],
    tasks=[research_task, write_task]
)

# Add Zubbl — one line
zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")
smart_crew = zubbl.wrap(crew)

# Use as normal
result = smart_crew.kickoff()

docs.title: Custom Agent
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient
from zubbl.types import TaskStatus

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")

# Manual trajectory recording
trajectory_id = zubbl.start_trajectory(
    task_description="Analyze customer feedback",
    metadata={"agent": "my_agent", "version": "1.0"}
)

# Record each step
zubbl.record_step(
    action="fetch_data",
    tool_name="database_query",
    tool_input={"query": "SELECT * FROM feedback"},
    tool_output="100 rows returned",
    tokens_used=150,
    latency_ms=200
)

zubbl.record_step(
    action="analyze_sentiment",
    tool_name="sentiment_analyzer",
    tool_input={"text": "..."},
    tool_output="positive: 60%, negative: 40%",
    tokens_used=500,
    latency_ms=1000
)

# End trajectory
trajectory = zubbl.end_trajectory(TaskStatus.SUCCESS)
print(f"Recorded: {trajectory.trajectory_id}")

docs.title: Query for Recommendations
docs.text:
Before starting a task, ask Zubbl what has worked before:

from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")

# Get policy recommendation
policy = zubbl.query(
    task_description="Write a Python sorting function",
    context={"language": "python"}
)

if policy:
    print(f"Confidence: {policy.confidence_score:.0%}")
    print(f"Recommended: {policy.recommended_actions}")
else:
    print("No policy yet — keep using Zubbl and it will learn!")

docs.title: Provide Feedback
docs.text:
Help Zubbl learn faster with explicit feedback:

from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")

# After seeing the result
zubbl.feedback(
    trajectory_id="traj_abc123",
    rating=0.8,  # -1.0 (bad) to 1.0 (good)
    comment="Great solution, but could be more efficient"
)

docs.title: Configuration Options
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient, RLMode

zubbl = ZubblClient(
    api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx",

    # API endpoint (default: production)
    base_url="https://api.zubbl.ai",

    # RL computation mode
    rl_mode=RLMode.LOCAL,      # Free — runs locally
    # rl_mode=RLMode.COREWEAVE,  # Cloud PRO — GPU powered
    # rl_mode=RLMode.B200,       # Enterprise

    # Automatic behaviors
    auto_ingest=True,   # Auto-ingest successful trajectories
    auto_query=True,    # Auto-query before each task

    # Performance
    timeout=30.0,       # API timeout in seconds
    local_cache=True,   # Cache policies locally
)

docs.title: Check Usage
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")

usage = zubbl.get_usage()
print(f"Trajectories: {usage.trajectories_ingested}")
print(f"Queries: {usage.queries_made}")
print(f"RL Cycles: {usage.rl_cycles_run}")
print(f"Credits remaining: {usage.credits_remaining}")
print(f"Success rate: {usage.task_success_rate:.0%}")

docs.title: Zubbl Wiki
docs.text:
Welcome to the Zubbl wiki — the Self-Evolving AI Agent SDK.

Make any AI agent 20-50% smarter — automatically, over time.

docs.title: Quick Links
docs.text: Page Description Home This page How It Works Dual learning loop overview Getting Started Installation & first integration SDK Reference Complete SDK API documentation API Reference REST API endpoints Configuration SDK configuration options

docs.title: What is Zubbl?
docs.text:
Zubbl fixes the biggest flaw in modern AI agents: they repeat the same mistakes indefinitely because they have no way to learn from experience.

Plug Zubbl into any existing AI agent platform and it creates a dual-loop learning system — combining trajectory learning (cross-task pattern extraction) with reflexion (per-task verbal self-correction) to improve automatically without any manual retraining.

docs.title: The Problem
docs.text:
Today's leading AI agent platforms are purely orchestration layers. They route tasks and chain tools together effectively, but they have no concept of learning from outcomes.

An agent that fails to complete a task on Monday will fail the exact same way on Friday, every week, indefinitely.

docs.title: The Solution — Dual Learning Loop
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="your-api-key")
agent = zubbl.wrap(your_agent)  # That's it. 3 lines.

Now your agent uses two complementary learning mechanisms:

Loop 1 — Trajectory Learning (cross-task): - Records every execution trajectory (steps, tools, outcomes) - Extracts reusable patterns from successful trajectories - Serves policy recommendations for similar future tasks - Learning transfers across tasks of the same type

Loop 2 — Reflexion (per-task, NeurIPS 2023): - When a task fails, the LLM generates a verbal self-reflection - Agent retries with the reflection in-context - Successful retries become new trajectories for Loop 1 - Immediate per-task recovery without waiting for cross-task patterns

Eval results: Baseline 82% -> Post-Zubbl 88% (+6 points) on HotPotQA hard reasoning tasks. 33% of failures recovered via reflexion.

Based on: Reflexion (NeurIPS 2023), ExpeL (AAAI 2024), ACE (ICLR 2026).

docs.title: Zubbl SDK Documentation
docs.text:
Version: 0.1.0 | Python: 3.9+ | License: MIT

Make any AI agent smarter automatically. Drop-in SDK that adds trajectory learning + failure recovery to any agent framework.

docs.title: Installation
docs.text:
pip install zubbl-sdk

Dependencies (installed automatically): httpx, pydantic, python-dotenv

docs.title: 3-Line Integration
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(your_agent)

# Use exactly like before — now it learns and self-recovers
result = smart_agent.run("Complete this task")

That's it. Your agent now: - Records every execution as a trajectory - Learns reusable patterns from successes - Auto-recovers from failures (retry, prompt patch, model fallback) - Gets policy recommendations before similar tasks

docs.title: ZubblClient
docs.text: The main entry point. All interactions go through this class.

docs.title: Constructor
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient, RLMode

client = ZubblClient(
    api_key="zubbl_yourtenantid_yoursecret",   # Required
    base_url="https://api.zubbl.ai",           # Default
    rl_mode=RLMode.LOCAL,                       # Free tier
    auto_ingest=True,                           # Auto-send trajectories
    auto_query=True,                            # Auto-fetch policies
    auto_recover=True,                          # Auto-recover failures
    timeout=30.0,                               # API timeout (seconds)
    retry_attempts=3,                           # API retry count
    local_cache=True,                           # Cache policies locally
    max_recovery_retries=3,                     # Max failure retries
    fallback_models=["gpt-4o-mini"],            # Fallback LLMs
)
Parameter Type Default Description api_key str required Your Zubbl API key (zubbl_<tenant>_<secret>) base_url str "https://api.zubbl.ai" API endpoint URL rl_mode RLMode LOCAL Learning compute mode auto_ingest bool True Automatically send trajectories to Zubbl auto_query bool True Automatically fetch policies before tasks auto_recover bool True Automatically attempt failure recovery timeout float 30.0 API request timeout in seconds retry_attempts int 3 Number of API retry attempts local_cache bool True Cache policies locally for faster lookups max_recovery_retries int 3 Max retries during failure recovery fallback_models List[str] [] Fallback LLM models for recovery

docs.title: Context Manager
docs.text:
# Sync
with ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx") as zubbl:
    smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(my_agent)
    result = smart_agent.run("task")

# Async
async with ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx") as zubbl:
    policy = await zubbl.query_async("task")

docs.title: wrap()
docs.text:
Wrap any agent to add automatic learning and failure recovery.

smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(agent, auto_recover=True)
Parameter Type Default Description agent Any required Any AI agent object auto_recover bool True Enable auto-recovery for this agent

Returns: ZubblWrappedAgent — Use exactly like the original agent.

The wrapped agent transparently proxies all method calls and attributes to the original agent. You can access the original via smart_agent.unwrapped.

docs.title: Framework Examples
docs.text:
LangGraph:

from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langgraph.prebuilt import create_react_agent
from zubbl import ZubblClient

model = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o")
agent = create_react_agent(model, [your_tools])

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(agent)

result = smart_agent.invoke({"messages": [("user", "Hello!")]})

CrewAI:

from crewai import Crew, Agent, Task
from zubbl import ZubblClient

crew = Crew(agents=[researcher, writer], tasks=[research_task, write_task])

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")
smart_crew = zubbl.wrap(crew)

result = smart_crew.kickoff()

Custom Agent:

class MyAgent:
    def run(self, task: str) -> str:
        # your agent logic
        return result

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(MyAgent())

result = smart_agent.run("Analyze this data")

docs.title: Manual Trajectory Recording
docs.text: For fine-grained control over what Zubbl captures, record trajectories manually instead of using wrap().

docs.title: start_trajectory()
docs.text:
Start recording a new trajectory.

traj_id = zubbl.start_trajectory(
    task_description="Analyze Q4 sales data",
    metadata={"source": "dashboard", "priority": "high"}
)
Parameter Type Description task_description str What the agent is trying to do metadata Dict Optional key-value metadata

Returns: str — Trajectory ID

docs.title: record_step()
docs.text:
Record each step the agent takes.

zubbl.record_step(
    action="search_database",
    tool_name="sql_executor",
    tool_input={"query": "SELECT * FROM sales WHERE quarter = 'Q4'"},
    tool_output="500 rows returned",
    reasoning="Need sales data for Q4 analysis",
    tokens_used=150,
    latency_ms=200
)
Parameter Type Default Description action str required What the agent did tool_name str None Tool/function used tool_input Dict None Input passed to the tool tool_output Any None Output from the tool reasoning str None Why the agent chose this action tokens_used int 0 Tokens consumed latency_ms int 0 Time taken in milliseconds

docs.title: end_trajectory()
docs.text:
Finish recording and optionally evaluate the result.

from zubbl.types import TaskStatus

trajectory = zubbl.end_trajectory(
    status=TaskStatus.SUCCESS,
    actual_output="Q4 revenue: $2.3M, up 15% YoY",
    expected_output="Q4 revenue summary",
    agent_confidence=0.9
)

print(f"Trajectory: {trajectory.trajectory_id}")
print(f"Total tokens: {trajectory.total_tokens}")
Parameter Type Default Description status TaskStatus required Final task status actual_output Any None What the agent produced expected_output Any None What was expected agent_confidence float None Agent's self-assessed confidence (0.0-1.0)

Returns: Trajectory — The completed trajectory object

docs.title: Full Manual Example
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient
from zubbl.types import TaskStatus

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")

# Start
traj_id = zubbl.start_trajectory(
    task_description="Summarize customer feedback",
    metadata={"agent": "support_bot", "version": "2.1"}
)

# Record steps
zubbl.record_step(
    action="fetch_feedback",
    tool_name="database_query",
    tool_input={"query": "SELECT * FROM feedback WHERE date > '2026-01-01'"},
    tool_output="342 rows",
    tokens_used=100,
    latency_ms=150
)

zubbl.record_step(
    action="analyze_sentiment",
    tool_name="sentiment_analyzer",
    tool_input={"text": "...batch of feedback..."},
    tool_output="positive: 65%, negative: 20%, neutral: 15%",
    tokens_used=800,
    latency_ms=2000
)

zubbl.record_step(
    action="generate_summary",
    tool_name="gpt-4o",
    tool_input={"prompt": "Summarize these sentiment results..."},
    tool_output="Overall customer sentiment is positive...",
    tokens_used=500,
    latency_ms=1500
)

# End
trajectory = zubbl.end_trajectory(
    status=TaskStatus.SUCCESS,
    actual_output="Overall customer sentiment is positive with 65% satisfaction..."
)

docs.title: Querying Policies
docs.text: Before starting a task, ask Zubbl what has worked before.

docs.title: query()
docs.text:
policy = zubbl.query(
    task_description="Write unit tests for the login function",
    context={"language": "python", "framework": "pytest"}
)

if policy:
    print(f"Confidence: {policy.confidence_score:.0%}")
    print(f"Recommended steps: {policy.recommended_actions}")
    print(f"Based on {len(policy.source_patterns)} patterns")
else:
    print("No policy yet — Zubbl will learn as you use it")
Parameter Type Description task_description str What the agent is about to do context Dict Optional additional context

Returns: Policy or None

docs.title: Async Version
docs.text:
policy = await zubbl.query_async(
    task_description="Debug the auth API",
    context={"error": "timeout on /login"}
)

docs.title: Submitting Feedback
docs.text: Help Zubbl learn faster with explicit feedback on trajectories.

docs.title: feedback()
docs.text:
result = zubbl.feedback(
    trajectory_id="traj_abc123",
    rating=0.8,
    comment="Good solution but could be more efficient"
)
Parameter Type Description trajectory_id str ID of the trajectory to rate rating float Score from -1.0 (terrible) to 1.0 (perfect) comment str Optional feedback text

Returns: Dict with feedback_id and status

docs.title: Validation
docs.text: Validate a trajectory's output after the fact.

docs.title: validate()
docs.text:
result = zubbl.validate(
    trajectory_id="traj_abc123",
    expected_output="Paris",
    actual_output="Paris, France",
    agent_confidence=0.95
)
Parameter Type Description trajectory_id str Trajectory to validate expected_output Any Ground truth / expected result actual_output Any What the agent actually produced agent_confidence float Agent's confidence (0.0-1.0)

Returns: Dict with validation result

docs.title: Failure Recovery
docs.text: Zubbl automatically intercepts and recovers from agent failures when auto_recover=True.

docs.title: How It Works
docs.text:
1. Your agent throws an error or returns a failure
  2. Zubbl classifies the failure (timeout, rate limit, empty response, etc.)
  3. Zubbl applies the best known fix (retry, prompt patch, model fallback, etc.)
  4. If recovery succeeds, the result is returned transparently
  5. Recovery data is recorded so Zubbl learns which fixes work

docs.title: Failure Categories
docs.text: Category Description Example EXCEPTION Unhandled error Python exception TIMEOUT Request took too long API timeout RATE_LIMIT Too many requests 429 response CONTEXT_OVERFLOW Input too large Token limit exceeded HALLUCINATION Incorrect output Wrong answer EMPTY_RESPONSE No output Blank response MALFORMED_OUTPUT Bad format Invalid JSON API_ERROR Provider error 500 response PARTIAL_FAILURE Incomplete result Missing fields

docs.title: Fix Types
docs.text: Fix When Used RETRY Transient errors RETRY_WITH_BACKOFF Rate limits PROMPT_PATCH Learned prompt improvements FALLBACK_MODEL Model-specific failures CACHED_RESPONSE Known good response exists INPUT_TRANSFORM Input format issues DECOMPOSE Task too complex SKIP Unrecoverable

docs.title: Checking Recovery Status
docs.text:
# After a wrapped agent call
recovery = zubbl.last_recovery

if recovery and recovery.success:
    print(f"Recovered using: {recovery.fix_applied}")
    print(f"Attempts: {recovery.attempts}")
    print(f"Original error: {recovery.original_error}")

docs.title: Recovery Statistics
docs.text:
stats = zubbl.get_failure_stats()
print(f"Total failures: {stats['total_failures']}")
print(f"Recovered: {stats['total_recovered']}")
print(f"Recovery rate: {stats['recovery_rate']:.0%}")

docs.title: Manual Access to Interceptor
docs.text:
interceptor = zubbl.interceptor
print(f"Known patterns: {interceptor.pattern_count}")
print(f"Cached responses: {interceptor.cached_responses}")

docs.title: Guardrails
docs.text: Set safety boundaries on agent behavior.

docs.title: Adding Guardrails
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient
from zubbl.types import GuardrailType, GuardrailAction, Guardrail

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")

# Limit max steps per task
zubbl.interceptor  # Access guardrail engine through the client

Available guardrail types:

Type Description MAX_STEPS Limit number of steps per task MAX_TOOL_CALLS Limit tool invocations MAX_TOKENS Cap token usage MAX_LATENCY_MS Timeout threshold REQUIRED_STEP Enforce a required action FORBIDDEN_TOOL Block specific tools OUTPUT_FORMAT Validate output format CUSTOM Custom validation logic

Actions on violation: WARN, BLOCK, FALLBACK, RETRY

docs.title: get_usage()
docs.text:
usage = zubbl.get_usage()

print(f"Trajectories ingested: {usage.trajectories_ingested}")
print(f"Queries made: {usage.queries_made}")
print(f"RL cycles run: {usage.rl_cycles_run}")
print(f"Credits used: {usage.credits_used}")
print(f"Credits remaining: {usage.credits_remaining}")
print(f"Success rate: {usage.task_success_rate:.0%}")
print(f"Avg improvement: {usage.avg_improvement:.0%}")

docs.title: LLM Adapter
docs.text: Zubbl includes a model-agnostic LLM adapter. Use it directly if you need multi-provider LLM access.

docs.title: Supported Providers
docs.text: Provider Models OpenAI gpt-4o, gpt-4o-mini, gpt-4-turbo, etc. Anthropic claude-4-sonnet, claude-4-opus, etc. Google gemini-pro, gemini-ultra Groq llama-3, mixtral (via OpenAI-compatible API) Ollama Any local model Together Various open models OpenRouter Multi-provider routing

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
from zubbl.llm import create_llm

# OpenAI (default)
llm = create_llm(provider="openai", model="gpt-4o-mini", api_key="sk-...")
response = llm.chat("What is 2 + 2?")
print(response)  # "4"

# Anthropic
llm = create_llm(provider="anthropic", model="claude-4-sonnet", api_key="sk-ant-...")
response = llm.chat("Explain recursion", system="You are a CS tutor")

# Ollama (local)
llm = create_llm(provider="ollama", model="llama3")
response = llm.chat("Hello!")

# Full control with messages
from zubbl.llm import Message

response = llm.generate([
    Message(role="system", content="You are a helpful assistant"),
    Message(role="user", content="Write a haiku about code")
])

print(response.content)        # The haiku
print(response.usage)          # {"prompt_tokens": 20, "completion_tokens": 15}
print(response.latency_ms)     # 450

docs.title: Async
docs.text:
response = await llm.generate_async([
    Message(role="user", content="Hello!")
])

docs.title: TaskStatus
docs.text:
from zubbl.types import TaskStatus

TaskStatus.SUCCESS    # Task completed successfully
TaskStatus.FAILURE    # Task failed
TaskStatus.PARTIAL    # Task partially completed
TaskStatus.TIMEOUT    # Task timed out

docs.title: RLMode
docs.text:
from zubbl import RLMode

RLMode.LOCAL       # Free — learning runs on your machine
RLMode.COREWEAVE   # Cloud PRO — GPU powered
RLMode.B200        # Enterprise — high-performance GPU

docs.title: Trajectory
docs.text:
Returned by end_trajectory().

trajectory.trajectory_id      # str — Unique ID
trajectory.tenant_id          # str — Your tenant ID
trajectory.task_description   # str — What was attempted
trajectory.steps              # List[Step] — Recorded steps
trajectory.status             # TaskStatus — Final status
trajectory.total_tokens       # int — Total tokens used
trajectory.total_latency_ms   # int — Total time taken
trajectory.metadata           # Dict — Custom metadata
trajectory.created_at         # datetime — When created
trajectory.is_successful      # bool — Convenience property

docs.title: Step
docs.text:
Each step in a trajectory.

step.step_id           # str — Unique step ID
step.action            # str — Action name
step.tool_name         # str | None — Tool used
step.tool_input        # Dict | None — Tool input
step.tool_output       # Any | None — Tool output
step.reasoning         # str | None — Agent reasoning
step.tokens_used       # int — Tokens for this step
step.latency_ms        # int — Time for this step
step.error             # str | None — Error message if failed
step.error_type        # str | None — Error classification

docs.title: Policy
docs.text:
Returned by query().

policy.policy_id             # str — Policy ID
policy.task_type             # str — Normalized task type
policy.recommended_actions   # List[str] — Suggested steps
policy.confidence_score      # float — 0.0 to 1.0
policy.source_patterns       # List[str] — Pattern IDs
policy.rl_mode               # RLMode — Learning mode used
policy.is_quarantined        # bool — If policy is disabled

docs.title: Feedback
docs.text:
feedback.feedback_id    # str — Feedback ID
feedback.trajectory_id  # str — Related trajectory
feedback.rating         # float — -1.0 to 1.0
feedback.comment        # str | None — Feedback text
feedback.source         # str — "human", "automated", "benchmark"

docs.title: UsageStats
docs.text:
usage.tenant_id              # str — Your tenant ID
usage.period                 # str — "day", "week", "month"
usage.trajectories_ingested  # int — Trajectories sent
usage.queries_made           # int — Policy queries
usage.rl_cycles_run          # int — Learning cycles completed
usage.credits_used           # int — Credits consumed
usage.credits_remaining      # int — Credits left
usage.task_success_rate      # float — Overall success rate
usage.avg_improvement        # float — Average improvement

docs.title: RecoveryResult
docs.text:
Returned by zubbl.last_recovery.

recovery.success           # bool — Did recovery work?
recovery.fix_applied       # FixType | None — What fix was used
recovery.attempts          # int — Number of attempts
recovery.original_error    # str | None — Original error
recovery.recovered_output  # Any | None — Recovered result
recovery.latency_ms        # int — Recovery time
recovery.pattern_id        # str | None — Matched pattern

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
Set these in your .env file or environment:

# Required
ZUBBL_API_KEY=zubbl_yourtenantid_yoursecret

# Optional — for LLM adapter
OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
ANTHROPIC_API_KEY=sk-ant-...

docs.title: API Key Format
docs.text:
zubbl_<tenant_id>_<secret>

Get your API key at zubbl.ai.

docs.title: Pricing
docs.text:
Tier Price What's Included Free $0/mo 10,000 cloud credits/month Local PRO $19/mo Unlimited local learning + 25K cloud credits Cloud PRO $49/mo 100,000 cloud credits (GPU powered) Team $199/mo Unlimited + 5 seats + priority support

Local learning is included free at every paid tier.

docs.title: Async Support
docs.text:
All API methods have async versions:

# Sync
policy = zubbl.query("task description")
result = zubbl.ingest(trajectory)

# Async
policy = await zubbl.query_async("task description")
result = await zubbl.ingest_async(trajectory)

docs.title: Error Handling
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient
from zubbl.types import TaskStatus

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx")

try:
    traj_id = zubbl.start_trajectory("risky task")
    # ... do work ...
    zubbl.end_trajectory(TaskStatus.SUCCESS)
except Exception as e:
    # Zubbl still records the failure
    zubbl.end_trajectory(TaskStatus.FAILURE)
    raise

docs.title: Complete Example
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient, RLMode
from zubbl.types import TaskStatus

# Initialize
zubbl = ZubblClient(
    api_key="zubbl_xxx_xxx",
    rl_mode=RLMode.LOCAL,
    auto_recover=True,
    fallback_models=["gpt-4o-mini"]
)

# Option A: Wrap an existing agent (automatic)
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(my_agent)
result = smart_agent.run("Write a Python sorting function")

# Option B: Manual recording (fine-grained control)
traj_id = zubbl.start_trajectory("Write a Python sorting function")

# Check if Zubbl has a recommendation
policy = zubbl.query("Write a Python sorting function")
if policy:
    print(f"Tip: {policy.recommended_actions}")

# Record steps
zubbl.record_step(
    action="generate_code",
    tool_name="gpt-4o",
    tool_input={"prompt": "Write a merge sort in Python"},
    tool_output="def merge_sort(arr): ...",
    tokens_used=200,
    latency_ms=800
)

zubbl.record_step(
    action="run_tests",
    tool_name="pytest",
    tool_input={"test_file": "test_sort.py"},
    tool_output="3 passed",
    tokens_used=0,
    latency_ms=500
)

# End trajectory
trajectory = zubbl.end_trajectory(
    TaskStatus.SUCCESS,
    actual_output="def merge_sort(arr): ...",
    agent_confidence=0.95
)

# Give feedback
zubbl.feedback(trajectory.trajectory_id, rating=1.0, comment="Clean implementation")

# Check usage
usage = zubbl.get_usage()
print(f"Success rate: {usage.task_success_rate:.0%}")
print(f"Credits remaining: {usage.credits_remaining}")

docs.title: Support
docs.text:
• Docs: docs.zubbl.ai
  • GitHub Issues: github.com/zubbl/zubbl/issues
  • Email: hello@zubbl.ai

docs.title: Added
docs.text:
• Skill Graph — skill dependency mapping and gap analysis
  • Vector Search — semantic trajectory similarity search
  • Training — continuous policy optimization
  • Real-time event streams
  • Billing integration
  • Dashboard: Billing, Skill Graph, Training, Streams, Vector Search pages

docs.title: Changed
docs.text:
• Parallelized dashboard API calls
  • Improved error messages
  • CORS: explicit origins instead of wildcard

docs.title: Fixed
docs.text:
• Webhook signature bypass when secret is empty
  • Overview page rendering improvements

docs.title: Initial Release
docs.text:
• ZubblClient SDK with wrap(), ingest(), query(), feedback()
  • Dual learning loop (trajectory + reflexion)
  • Dashboard (overview, trajectories, APO, guardrails)

docs.title: Authentication
docs.text: All API requests require authentication via Bearer token.

docs.title: API Key Format
docs.text: Authorization: Bearer zubbl_<tenant_id>_<secret>

docs.title: Getting an API Key
docs.text:
1. Sign up at zubbl.in
  2. Go to Settings → API Keys
  3. Click Generate New Key
  4. Save the key — it's shown only once

docs.title: Example Request
docs.text:
curl -H "Authorization: Bearer zubbl_acme_abc123" \
  https://api.zubbl.in/health

from zubbl import ZubblClient
zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_acme_abc123")

docs.title: API Endpoints
docs.text:
Base URL: https://api.zubbl.in

All requests require authentication via Bearer token: 

Authorization: Bearer zubbl_<your_key>

docs.title: Health Check
docs.text:
GET /health
Returns API status. No auth required.

docs.title: Ingest Trajectory
docs.text:
POST /ingest
Record an agent execution for learning.

{
  "trajectory_id": "traj-001",
  "tenant_id": "your_tenant_id",
  "task_description": "Review code for security issues",
  "steps": [
    {
      "action": "scan",
      "tool_name": "semgrep",
      "tool_input": {"rules": "owasp"},
      "tool_output": "2 findings"
    }
  ],
  "status": "success",
  "total_latency_ms": 3200
}

docs.title: Query Policy
docs.text:
GET /query?task=code_review
Get recommended strategies for a task type.

docs.title: Submit Feedback
docs.text:
POST /feedback
Rate a trajectory to improve learning.

{
  "feedback_id": "fb-001",
  "trajectory_id": "traj-001",
  "rating": 0.9,
  "source": "human",
  "comment": "Excellent review"
}

docs.title: Usage Stats
docs.text:
GET /usage
Credit balance, trajectory counts, and success rate.

docs.title: Improvement Score
docs.text:
GET /improvement
How much the SDK improved your agent performance over time.

docs.title: Skill Graph (Cloud Pro+)
docs.text: Endpoint Method Description /graph/stats GET Node/relationship counts /graph/skills GET List skills /graph/skills POST Create a skill /graph/recommendations/{task} GET Pattern recommendations /graph/skill-gaps/{agent}/{task} GET Skill gap analysis

docs.title: Vector Search (Cloud Pro+)
docs.text: Endpoint Method Description /vector/similar GET Find similar past trajectories /vector/patterns GET Find successful patterns

docs.title: Training (Cloud Pro+)
docs.text: Endpoint Method Description /training/stats GET Training status /training/trigger POST Trigger training run

docs.title: Billing
docs.text: Endpoint Method Description /billing/checkout POST Create upgrade checkout /billing/subscription GET Current subscription

docs.title: Error Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 200 Success 400 Invalid request 401 Missing or invalid API key 429 Rate limited 500 Server error

docs.title: Interactive Docs
docs.text:
• Swagger UI: api.zubbl.in/docs
  • ReDoc: api.zubbl.in/redoc

docs.title: HTTP Status Codes
docs.text: Code Meaning 200 Success 201 Created 400 Bad request — invalid input 401 Unauthorized — invalid or missing API key 403 Forbidden — insufficient plan 404 Not found 429 Rate limited — slow down 500 Internal server error 503 Service unavailable

docs.title: Error Response Format
docs.text:
{
  "detail": "Human-readable error message"
}

docs.title: Rate Limits
docs.text:
Plan Requests/min Free 100 Local Pro 1,000 Cloud Pro 5,000 Team 10,000

When rate limited, the response includes: 

X-RateLimit-Remaining: 0
Retry-After: 30

docs.title: Webhooks
docs.text: Receive real-time notifications when events occur.

docs.title: Registering a Webhook
docs.text:
zubbl.register_webhook(
    url="https://your-server.com/zubbl-webhook",
    events=["trajectory.completed", "failure.detected", "policy.updated"],
)

docs.title: Events
docs.text: Event Fires When trajectory.completed A trajectory finishes processing failure.detected An agent failure is detected policy.updated Policies are recalculated training.completed A training run finishes

docs.title: Payload Format
docs.text:
{
  "event": "trajectory.completed",
  "timestamp": "2026-03-21T10:00:00Z",
  "data": {
    "trajectory_id": "traj-001",
    "status": "success",
    "patterns_extracted": 3
  }
}

docs.title: Signature Verification
docs.text:
All webhooks include an X-Zubbl-Signature header for verification:

import hmac, hashlib

def verify(body: bytes, signature: str, secret: str) -> bool:
    digest = hmac.new(secret.encode(), body, hashlib.sha256).hexdigest()
    return hmac.compare_digest(digest, signature)

docs.title: Environment Variables
docs.text:
export ZUBBL_API_KEY="zubbl_your_tenant_yoursecret"
export ZUBBL_BASE_URL="https://api.zubbl.in"  # optional

docs.title: Client Options
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(
    api_key="zubbl_xxx",
    base_url="https://api.zubbl.in",
    timeout=30,           # request timeout in seconds
    max_retries=3,        # retry on transient failures
    rl_mode="cloud",      # "local" | "cloud" | "hybrid"
)

docs.title: RL Modes
docs.text: Mode Description Best For local Pattern matching + reflexion on your machine Development, testing cloud Full server-side learning and training Production hybrid Local reflexion + cloud trajectory learning Cost-sensitive production

docs.title: .env File
docs.text:
Create a .env file in your project root:

ZUBBL_API_KEY=zubbl_your_tenant_yoursecret
ZUBBL_BASE_URL=https://api.zubbl.in
ZUBBL_RL_MODE=cloud

The SDK auto-loads .env via python-dotenv.

docs.title: From PyPI (Recommended)
docs.text: pip install zubbl-sdk

docs.title: From Source
docs.text:
git clone https://github.com/zubbl/zubbl.git
cd zubbl
pip install -e .

docs.title: Requirements
docs.text:
• Python 3.9+
  • Dependencies (installed automatically): httpx, pydantic, python-dotenv

docs.title: Verify Installation
docs.text:
import zubbl
print(zubbl.__version__)

docs.title: Quick Start
docs.text: Get Zubbl running in your project in under 5 minutes.

docs.title: 1. Get Your API Key
docs.text: Sign up at app.zubbl.tech and save the API key shown after registration.

docs.title: 2. Install
docs.text: pip install zubbl-sdk

docs.title: 3. Wrap Your Agent (Simplest)
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(
    api_key="zubbl_YOUR_KEY",
    base_url="https://api.zubbl.tech",
)

# Wrap any callable — Zubbl handles everything automatically
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(your_agent)
result = smart_agent.run("Analyze this code for bugs")

docs.title: 4. Manual Integration (More Control)
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(
    api_key="zubbl_YOUR_KEY",
    base_url="https://api.zubbl.tech",
)

# Before task — get policy recommendations
policy = zubbl.query(task_description="code review")
if policy:
    print(f"Recommended approach: {policy.task_type}")
    print(f"Confidence: {policy.confidence_score}")

# Start recording a trajectory
trajectory_id = zubbl.start_trajectory(
    task_description="Review PR #42 for security issues",
)

# Record each step your agent takes
zubbl.record_step(
    action="read_file",
    tool_name="file_reader",
    tool_input={"file": "auth.py"},
    tool_output="def login(user, pwd): ...",
    tokens_used=150,
    latency_ms=200,
)

zubbl.record_step(
    action="analyze",
    tool_name="security_scanner",
    tool_input={"check": "sql_injection"},
    tool_output="No SQL injection found",
    tokens_used=300,
    latency_ms=1200,
)

# End and auto-ingest the trajectory
trajectory = zubbl.end_trajectory(status="success")

# Submit feedback to improve learning
zubbl.feedback(
    trajectory_id=trajectory_id,
    rating=0.9,       # -1.0 (terrible) to 1.0 (perfect)
    comment="Fast and accurate review",
)

docs.title: 5. Check Your Dashboard
docs.text:
# Check usage
usage = zubbl.get_usage()
print(f"Trajectories: {usage.trajectories_ingested}")
print(f"Credits remaining: {usage.credits_remaining}")
print(f"Success rate: {usage.task_success_rate:.1%}")

Or visit app.zubbl.tech to see everything visually.

docs.title: What Happens Behind the Scenes
docs.text:
1. Your trajectory is stored and patterns are extracted
  2. Patterns are scored and policies are updated automatically
  3. Next time you call query(), you get improved recommendations
  4. If a task fails, reflexion kicks in automatically for retry

docs.title: Next Steps
docs.text:
• Full Example — complete working script
  • Configuration — customize SDK behavior
  • SDK Reference — all methods documented

docs.title: CrewAI Integration
docs.text: Integrate Zubbl with CrewAI multi-agent systems.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text: pip install zubbl-sdk crewai

docs.title: Wrapping a CrewAI Crew
docs.text:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx")

# Define your agents and tasks
researcher = Agent(role="Researcher", ...)
writer = Agent(role="Writer", ...)
crew = Crew(agents=[researcher, writer], tasks=[...])

# Wrap the crew
smart_crew = zubbl.wrap(crew)
result = smart_crew.kickoff()

docs.title: Per-Agent Tracking
docs.text:
# Wrap individual agents for granular tracking
smart_researcher = zubbl.wrap(researcher, agent_id="researcher-001")
smart_writer = zubbl.wrap(writer, agent_id="writer-001")

crew = Crew(agents=[smart_researcher, smart_writer], tasks=[...])

docs.title: Full Example
docs.text: A complete working script showing the Zubbl SDK end-to-end.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text: pip install zubbl-sdk

docs.title: Complete Script
docs.text:
"""
Zubbl SDK — Complete Example
Run: python example.py
"""
from zubbl import ZubblClient

# 1. Initialize
zubbl = ZubblClient(
    api_key="zubbl_YOUR_TENANT_YOUR_SECRET",  # Get from https://app.zubbl.tech
    base_url="https://api.zubbl.tech",
)

# 2. Check usage
usage = zubbl.get_usage()
print(f"Trajectories: {usage.trajectories_ingested}")
print(f"Credits: {usage.credits_remaining}")


# 3. Query for recommendations before running a task
policy = zubbl.query(task_description="code review")
if policy:
    print(f"Recommended approach: {policy.task_type}")
    print(f"Confidence: {policy.confidence_score}")
else:
    print("No policies yet — they build up as you ingest more trajectories")


# 4. Record a trajectory (your agent's execution)
trajectory_id = zubbl.start_trajectory(
    task_description="Review main.py for code quality issues",
    metadata={"project": "my-app"},
)

zubbl.record_step(
    action="read_file",
    tool_name="file_reader",
    tool_input={"file": "main.py"},
    tool_output="def hello(): print('world')",
    tokens_used=100,
    latency_ms=200,
)

zubbl.record_step(
    action="analyze",
    tool_name="ast_parser",
    tool_input={"check": "code_quality"},
    tool_output="No issues found",
    tokens_used=250,
    latency_ms=800,
)

zubbl.record_step(
    action="respond",
    tool_name="output",
    tool_input={"format": "markdown"},
    tool_output="Code looks clean. No issues detected.",
    tokens_used=100,
    latency_ms=300,
)

# End trajectory — auto-ingests to Zubbl for learning
trajectory = zubbl.end_trajectory(status="success")
print(f"Trajectory {trajectory_id} ingested!")


# 5. Submit feedback
zubbl.feedback(
    trajectory_id=trajectory_id,
    rating=0.85,
    comment="Accurate review, fast execution",
)
print("Feedback submitted!")


# 6. Check updated usage
usage = zubbl.get_usage()
print(f"Trajectories: {usage.trajectories_ingested}")
print(f"Success rate: {usage.task_success_rate:.1%}")


# 7. Check failure stats
stats = zubbl.get_failure_stats()
print(f"Failures seen: {stats['total_failures_seen']}")
print(f"Auto-recovered: {stats['total_auto_recovered']}")


print("\nDone! Check your dashboard at https://app.zubbl.tech")

docs.title: Wrap an Existing Agent (Even Simpler)
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_YOUR_KEY")

# Wrap any callable — Zubbl handles everything automatically
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(your_existing_agent)
result = smart_agent.run("Your task here")

# That's it — trajectories are recorded, policies are applied,
# failures trigger reflexion, and your agent improves over time.

docs.title: LangChain
docs.text:
from langchain.agents import create_openai_tools_agent
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_YOUR_KEY")
agent = create_openai_tools_agent(llm, tools, prompt)
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(agent)
result = smart_agent.invoke({"input": "Analyze this codebase"})

docs.title: CrewAI
docs.text:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_YOUR_KEY")
crew = Crew(agents=[researcher, writer], tasks=[task1, task2])
smart_crew = zubbl.wrap(crew)
result = smart_crew.kickoff()

docs.title: LangChain Integration
docs.text: Integrate Zubbl with LangChain agents.

docs.title: Setup
docs.text: pip install zubbl-sdk langchain

docs.title: Wrapping a LangChain Agent
docs.text:
from langchain.agents import create_openai_tools_agent
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx")

# Create your LangChain agent
agent = create_openai_tools_agent(llm, tools, prompt)

# Wrap it with Zubbl
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(agent)

# Use as normal — Zubbl records and learns
result = smart_agent.invoke({"input": "Analyze this codebase"})

docs.title: Manual Integration
docs.text:
# Get recommendations before running
policies = zubbl.query(task_type="code_analysis")

# Inject into agent prompt
enhanced_prompt = f"{base_prompt}\n\nRecommended strategies:\n"
for p in policies:
    enhanced_prompt += f"- {p.pattern}: {p.description}\n"

# Run and record
result = agent.invoke({"input": task})
zubbl.ingest(
    trajectory_id=str(uuid4()),
    task_description=task,
    steps=extract_steps(result),
    status="success" if result else "failure",
)

docs.title: Wrap Your Agent
docs.text: The simplest way to integrate Zubbl — wrap any callable agent.

docs.title: Basic Wrapping
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(api_key="zubbl_xxx")

# Wrap any callable
smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(my_agent)

# Use exactly like before
result = smart_agent.run("Review this pull request")

docs.title: What Wrapping Does
docs.text:
1. Before execution — queries Zubbl for policy recommendations
  2. During execution — records steps, tools used, tokens, latency
  3. After execution — ingests the trajectory for learning
  4. On failure — triggers reflexion and retries automatically

docs.title: Custom Step Recording
docs.text:
with zubbl.trace("code_review") as trace:
    trace.step("read_file", tool="file_reader", output=content)
    trace.step("analyze", tool="ast_parser", output=analysis)
    trace.complete(status="success")

docs.title: ZubblClient
docs.text: The main entry point for the Zubbl SDK.

docs.title: Initialize
docs.text:
from zubbl import ZubblClient

zubbl = ZubblClient(
    api_key="zubbl_YOUR_KEY",
    base_url="https://api.zubbl.tech",  # default
    timeout=30,                          # request timeout in seconds
    max_retries=3,                       # retry on transient failures
    rl_mode="cloud",                     # "local" | "cloud" | "hybrid"
)

docs.title: start_trajectory(task_description, metadata=None) → str
docs.text:
Start recording a new trajectory. Returns the trajectory ID.

tid = zubbl.start_trajectory(
    task_description="Summarize quarterly report",
    metadata={"source": "automated"},
)

docs.title: record_step(action, tool_name=None, tool_input=None, tool_output=None, reasoning=None, tokens_used=0, latency_ms=0)
docs.text:
Record a step in the current trajectory.

zubbl.record_step(
    action="read_file",
    tool_name="doc_reader",
    tool_input={"file": "report.pdf"},
    tool_output="Revenue: $2.3M",
    tokens_used=150,
    latency_ms=800,
)

docs.title: end_trajectory(status, actual_output=None, expected_output=None, agent_confidence=None) → Trajectory
docs.text:
End and auto-ingest the current trajectory. Status must be "success", "failure", "partial", or "timeout".

traj = zubbl.end_trajectory(status="success")

docs.title: query(task_description, context=None) → Policy | None
docs.text:
Get policy recommendations before running a task.

policy = zubbl.query(task_description="code review")
if policy:
    print(policy.task_type)           # str
    print(policy.confidence_score)    # float
    print(policy.recommended_actions) # list
    print(policy.is_quarantined)      # bool

docs.title: feedback(trajectory_id, rating, comment=None) → dict
docs.text:
Submit feedback on a trajectory. Rating is -1.0 to 1.0.

zubbl.feedback(
    trajectory_id="traj_abc123",
    rating=0.85,
    comment="Accurate and fast",
)

docs.title: get_usage() → UsageStats
docs.text:
Get usage statistics.

usage = zubbl.get_usage()
print(usage.trajectories_ingested)  # int
print(usage.credits_remaining)      # int
print(usage.task_success_rate)      # float (0.0-1.0)
print(usage.queries_made)           # int
print(usage.rl_cycles_run)          # int

docs.title: wrap(agent, auto_recover=None) → WrappedAgent
docs.text:
Wrap any callable agent for automatic trajectory recording and recovery.

smart_agent = zubbl.wrap(my_agent)
result = smart_agent.run("Your task")

docs.title: get_failure_stats() → dict
docs.text:
Get failure statistics and recovery rates.

stats = zubbl.get_failure_stats()
print(stats["total_failures_seen"])
print(stats["total_auto_recovered"])
print(stats["recovery_rate"])

docs.title: validate(trajectory_id, expected_output, actual_output=None, agent_confidence=0.0) → dict
docs.text:
Validate a trajectory's output against expected results.

result = zubbl.validate(
    trajectory_id="traj_abc123",
    expected_output="Summary of Q4 earnings",
    actual_output="Q4 revenue grew 15%",
    agent_confidence=0.9,
)

docs.title: find_similar_trajectories(query_text, top_k=5, status_filter=None) → list
docs.text:
Find similar past trajectories using vector search.

results = zubbl.find_similar_trajectories(
    query_text="fix authentication bug",
    top_k=5,
    status_filter="success",
)
for r in results:
    print(r.trajectory_id, r.score)

docs.title: get_graph_recommendations(task_type, min_confidence=0.5, limit=5) → list
docs.text:
Get skill graph recommendations.

recs = zubbl.get_graph_recommendations(task_type="code_review")
for r in recs:
    print(r.pattern, r.confidence, r.description)

docs.title: get_skill_gaps(agent_id, task_type) → list
docs.text:
Analyze skill gaps for an agent.

gaps = zubbl.get_skill_gaps(agent_id="my-agent", task_type="security_audit")
for g in gaps:
    print(g.skill, g.importance)

docs.title: get_training_stats() → TrainingStats | None
docs.text:
Get training pipeline status.

stats = zubbl.get_training_stats()
if stats:
    print(stats.model_name)
    print(stats.training_runs)
    print(stats.trajectories_buffered)

docs.title: trigger_training() → dict | None
docs.text:
Manually trigger a training run.

zubbl.trigger_training()

docs.title: get_services_health() → ServiceHealth
docs.text:
Check which cloud services are available.

health = zubbl.get_services_health()
print(health.status)

docs.title: get_streams_info() → dict | None
docs.text:
Get streaming queue status.

info = zubbl.get_streams_info()

docs.title: Async Methods
docs.text:
All core methods have async variants:

# Async versions
await zubbl.ingest_async(trajectory)
policy = await zubbl.query_async(task_description="...")
results = await zubbl.find_similar_trajectories_async(query_text="...")
recs = await zubbl.get_graph_recommendations_async(task_type="...")

docs.title: Feedback
docs.text: Submit feedback on trajectories to improve learning.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
zubbl.feedback(
    trajectory_id="traj-001",
    rating=0.9,         # -1.0 (terrible) to 1.0 (perfect)
    source="human",     # "human" | "automated" | "llm"
    comment="Fast and accurate review",
)

docs.title: Rating Scale
docs.text: Range Meaning 0.8 to 1.0 Excellent 0.5 to 0.8 Good 0.0 to 0.5 Acceptable -0.5 to 0.0 Poor -1.0 to -0.5 Terrible

docs.title: Sources
docs.text:
• human — Manual review by a person
  • automated — Automated test/validation
  • llm — LLM-based evaluation

docs.title: Policy Queries
docs.text: Query Zubbl for optimized task strategies before execution.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
policies = zubbl.query(
    task_type="code_review",
    limit=5,
)

for policy in policies:
    print(f"{policy.pattern}: {policy.score}")

docs.title: How Policies Work
docs.text:
1. Zubbl analyzes all past trajectories for the given task type
  2. Patterns are scored by success rate, efficiency, and feedback
  3. Top patterns are returned as policy recommendations
  4. Your agent uses these to guide its approach

docs.title: Scoring Factors
docs.text: Factor Weight Description Success Rate 40% Historical success of the pattern Token Efficiency 30% Lower token usage = better Latency 20% Faster execution = better Feedback 10% Human/automated ratings

docs.title: Skill Graph
docs.text:
Query and manage the skill dependency graph.

Cloud Pro+ Feature

Skill Graph requires Cloud Pro ($99/mo) or Team ($299/mo) plan.

docs.title: List Skills
docs.text:
skills = zubbl.graph_skills(domain="engineering")
for skill in skills:
    print(f"{skill['name']}: {skill['proficiency']}")

docs.title: Get Recommendations
docs.text:
recs = zubbl.graph_recommendations(
    task_type="code_review",
    min_confidence=0.7,
    limit=5,
)

docs.title: Skill Gap Analysis
docs.text:
gaps = zubbl.skill_gaps(
    agent_id="agent-001",
    task_type="security_audit",
)
print(f"Coverage: {gaps['coverage']}")
for skill in gaps['missing_skills']:
    print(f"  Missing: {skill['skill']} (importance: {skill['importance']})")

docs.title: Training
docs.text:
Monitor and trigger training runs.

Cloud Pro+ Feature

Training requires Cloud Pro ($99/mo) or Team ($299/mo) plan.

docs.title: Check Training Status
docs.text:
stats = zubbl.training_stats()
print(f"Model: {stats['model_name']}")
print(f"Buffered: {stats['trajectories_buffered']}/{stats['buffer_threshold']}")
print(f"Total runs: {stats['training_runs']}")

docs.title: Trigger Training
docs.text:
result = zubbl.trigger_training()
print(f"Training triggered: {result}")

Training runs automatically when the buffer reaches the threshold (default: 50 trajectories).

docs.title: Trajectory Recording
docs.text: Record agent execution trajectories for learning.

docs.title: Ingesting Trajectories
docs.text:
zubbl.ingest(
    trajectory_id="traj-001",
    task_description="Analyze codebase for security issues",
    steps=[
        {"action": "read_file", "tool_name": "file_reader", "tool_output": "..."},
        {"action": "analyze", "tool_name": "ast_parser", "tool_output": "..."},
    ],
    status="success",  # "success" | "failure" | "partial"
    metadata={"tokens": 1240, "duration_ms": 3200},
)

docs.title: IngestRequest Fields
docs.text: Field Type Required Description trajectory_id string Yes Unique ID for the trajectory tenant_id string Auto Extracted from API key task_description string Yes What the agent was doing steps list[Step] Yes Ordered list of actions taken status string Yes Outcome: success, failure, partial metadata dict No Extra data (tokens, duration, etc.)

docs.title: Step Schema
docs.text: Field Type Description action string What the agent did tool_name string Tool used (if any) tool_input string Input to the tool tool_output string Output from the tool reasoning string Agent's reasoning (if captured)

docs.title: Vector Search
docs.text:
Find similar past trajectories using semantic search.

Cloud Pro+ Feature

Vector Search requires Cloud Pro ($99/mo) or Team ($299/mo) plan.

docs.title: Search Similar Trajectories
docs.text:
results = zubbl.search_similar(
    text="fix authentication bug in login flow",
    top_k=5,
    status_filter="success",
)
for r in results:
    print(f"{r['trajectory_id']}: score={r['score']:.2f}")

docs.title: Search Patterns
docs.text:
patterns = zubbl.search_patterns(
    text="retry with backoff",
    top_k=5,
    min_success_rate=0.8,
)
